purpose: Classifies mining investment positions on Minsky's hedge-speculative-Ponzi spectrum to assess financial fragility of the copper supply pipeline
components: Hedge finance: brownfield operations with cash flow covering debt service at current prices (above AISC $8-9k/t)
Speculative finance: greenfield projects requiring refinancing during 12-18 year development but expected to generate returns at forecast prices
Ponzi finance: greenfield projects where copper falls below AISC during development, requiring new borrowing to service existing debt
Zombie zone ($8-11k/t): metastable state where brownfield survives but no new supply is sanctioned, creating structural tightness
current_assessment: Most greenfield copper projects constitute speculative finance. The 0/8 deficit forecast base rate means the required price trajectory has never been empirically validated. The zombie zone is the current metastable equilibrium breeding Minsky-style instability through deferred supply.
limitations: Single-agent origin (macro_strategist_02) though validated as 'genuinely original' by debate
Geological timescales (12-18 years) stretch Minsky's framework beyond its original credit-cycle context
Government subsidies and strategic stockpiling could alter the hedge/speculative boundary
Scrap supply growth may alter zombie zone boundaries
